In `analyzeData`, the resource is only closed if both `b > 0` and `level > 10` are true. Since `level` is passed as `0` in the call `analyzeData(fileStream, 0)`, the condition `level > 10` is false, so the `close` branch is not taken. The resource is not closed elsewhere along this path, and there is no explicit close, try-with-resources, or ownership transfer by return/argument after this point. The path is feasible without branch conflicts.
